title: Import AI 453: Breaking AI agents; MirrorCode; gradual disempowerment
summary: METR and Epoch's MirrorCode benchmark demonstrates AI systems can reverse-engineer complex software with thousands of lines of code. Google DeepMind outlines six genres of attacks against AI agents and necessary mitigations across technical, ecosystem, and legal frameworks.

title: Transformers to MLX: efficient inference on Apple Silicon
summary: Discusses converting Transformers models to MLX format, enabling efficient inference on Apple Silicon devices.

title: Training and Finetuning Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
summary: Comprehensive guide on training multimodal embedding and reranker models using Sentence Transformers library for improved semantic search and ranking tasks.

title: My bets on open models, mid-2026
summary: Details key beliefs about open model development including technical strength of open labs, focus on benchmark scores versus robustness, dominance in repetitive automation tasks, and funding challenges for Chinese open-weight companies.

title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
summary: Google DeepMind introduced a new audio model with granular audio tags that give precise control for directing AI-generated speech with enhanced expressiveness.

title: Rethinking AI TCO: Why Cost per Token Is the Only Metric That Matters
summary: NVIDIA argues enterprises should evaluate AI infrastructure on cost per token rather than compute cost or FLOPS per dollar. Blackwell delivers 35x lower cost per million tokens compared to Hopper.

title: Inside VAKRA: Reasoning, Tool Use, and Failure Modes of Agents
summary: In-depth analysis of the VAKRA benchmark examining how AI agents reason, utilize tools, and fail, providing insights into agent behavior and limitations.
